steps.annotation: Body executes because the condition was true

teachMe: score == 50 is true, so the if-body runs and prints pass.
pin == 0 is false (1234 != 0), else branch runs, prints locked.
x == 5 true -> prints yes; then unconditional cout prints done.
n == 8 is false (7 != 8), else branch runs.
code == 42 is true. Body runs, prints match.
Two variables can be == compared. 10 == 10 true.

clozeSentence: Test for equality with the ___ operator
explanation: == tests equality (don't confuse with =, the assignment operator).

clozeSentence: PIN match uses the ___ operator
explanation: == compares two values. = would assign and is wrong here.

clozeSentence: Two variables compare with the ___ operator
explanation: == checks if a equals b.

spec: Write an if header that runs when score equals 50 (just the if line, ending with the open brace)
explanation: == tests equality. = would assign and would be wrong.

spec: Write an if-block that prints "exact" when score == 50. Use cout. Three lines (open brace on line 1, cout on line 2, close brace on line 3).
explanation: == compares; cout prints when condition holds.

spec: Write a complete program that declares int pin = 1234, then prints "unlocked" if pin == 1234 else "locked"
explanation: Full if-else with the equality operator on int pin.

examples.label: Example A: equality test for score
Example B: equality test for pin

prompt: By the same pattern, write an if that prints "match" when code equals 42.

steps.annotation: Body runs because condition was true

teachMe: age != 17 -> 18 != 17 true. if-body runs, prints adult.
age != 17 -> 17 != 17 false. else runs, prints seventeen.

clozeSentence: Use the ___ operator to test "not equal"
explanation: != is the not-equal operator.

clozeSentence: Exclude the value 17 with the ___ operator
explanation: age != 17 is true for everything except 17.

clozeSentence: Two values differ when the ___ operator is true
explanation: != returns true when the operands are not equal.

spec: Write an if header that runs when x is not equal to 0 (just the if line ending with the open brace)
explanation: != is the not-equal operator.

spec: Write an if-block that prints "differ" when a != b. Three lines.
explanation: Body runs only when the operands are not equal.

prompt: By the same pattern, write an if that prints "differ" when a is not equal to b.

steps.annotation: < is strict; 17 < 18 is true. 18 < 18 would be FALSE
i < 3 is the loop guard. STRICT < means i = 3 stops the loop

teachMe: BOUNDARY trap: 18 < 18 is FALSE (strict <). else branch runs.
-5 < 0 true. Body runs and prints negative.

clozeSentence: Strict less-than uses the ___ operator
explanation: < is strict. 18 < 18 is false.

clozeSentence: The standard for-loop guard uses the ___ operator
explanation: i < n is the canonical guard.

clozeSentence: Detect negative values with the ___ operator
explanation: x < 0 is true for any negative number.

spec: Write an if header that runs when age is strictly less than 18 (just the if line ending with the open brace)
explanation: Strict <. 18 itself does not match.

spec: Write an if-block that prints "too young" when age < 18. Three lines.
explanation: Strict less-than test.

spec: Write a complete program that declares int age = 17 and prints "too young" if age < 18 else "can vote"
explanation: Full voting-age check using strict <.

prompt: Write an if-block that prints "too young" when age is strictly less than 18

steps.annotation: > is strict; checks if x beats current max

clozeSentence: Strict greater-than uses the ___ operator
explanation: > is strict. 500 > 500 is false.

clozeSentence: Max-finder pattern uses the ___ operator
explanation: x > max_val updates the running maximum.

clozeSentence: Detect strictly positive values with the ___ operator
explanation: n > 0 is true only for positive numbers.

spec: Write an if header that runs when amount is strictly greater than 500 (just the if line)
explanation: Strict >. 500 itself does not match.

spec: Write an if-block that updates max_val to x when x > max_val. Three lines.
explanation: Max-finder pattern.

prompt: By the same pattern, write an if that prints "A band" when score is strictly greater than 80.

clozeSentence: Inclusive less-than-or-equal uses the ___ operator
explanation: <= is inclusive at the boundary. 10 <= 10 is true.

clozeSentence: Inclusive upper bound uses the ___ operator
explanation: i <= count includes count itself.

clozeSentence: Test for zero or negative with the ___ operator
explanation: t <= 0 catches zero AND negative values.

spec: Write an if header that runs when n is less than or equal to 10 (just the if line)
explanation: <= is inclusive.

spec: Write an if-block that prints "in range" when n <= 10. Three lines.
explanation: Inclusive boundary check.

spec: Write an if-block that prints "OK" when reds is less than or equal to 3. Three lines.
explanation: Inclusive limit using <=.

prompt: By the same pattern, write an if that prints "non-positive" when t is less than or equal to 0.

clozeSentence: Inclusive greater-than-or-equal uses the ___ operator
explanation: >= is inclusive. 50 >= 50 is true.

clozeSentence: Voting age check (18 included) uses the ___ operator
explanation: >= 18 includes age 18 itself.

clozeSentence: Detect non-negative values with the ___ operator
explanation: x >= 0 is true for zero and positive values.

spec: Write an if header that runs when score is greater than or equal to 50 (just the if line)
explanation: >= is inclusive.

spec: Write an if-block that prints "pass" when score >= 50. Three lines.
explanation: Score-grade pass mark using inclusive >=.

steps.annotation: Test the bool directly. true triggers body

levelLabel: L5 -> comparison returns bool (storing the test)

steps.annotation: >= produces a bool. passed is true

teachMe: x == 5 evaluates to true. match holds true. if(match) is true.
>= returns bool. cout prints true as 1.
< returns bool. small = true. cout prints 1.
== bool stored in same. if(same) runs body.
> returns bool. big = true. if-body runs.

clozeSentence: A comparison returns a value of type ___
explanation: All comparisons (==, !=, <, >, <=, >=) return a bool.

code: bool passed = ___score >= 50);
clozeSentence: Wrap the comparison so it can be assigned; the opener is ___
explanation: Parens make the comparison a single boolean expression.

clozeSentence: An if can test a bool variable directly; the test is ___
explanation: if (small) is true when small holds true.

spec: Write one line storing the result of x == 5 in a bool called match
explanation: == returns a bool that can be stored.

spec: Declare int score = 75, then bool passed = (score >= 50). Two lines.
explanation: Comparison stored in a bool variable.

prompt: By the same pattern, store the result of n < 10 into a bool called small.

question: Type the line that stores x == 5 into a bool called match
code: bool match = (x == 5);

steps.annotation: && requires BOTH true. 10 > 5 true AND 10 < 20 true
Body runs because both conditions hold
x is zero — division would crash
&& SHORT-CIRCUITS: left is false, RIGHT NEVER EVALUATED. No crash.

teachMe: 10 > 5 true && 10 < 20 true -> overall true. Body runs.
25 > 5 true BUT 25 < 20 false. && needs both. else runs.
3 > 5 false. && SHORT-CIRCUITS: right side never evaluated. Result false. else runs.
a==1 true, b==1 false. && fails. else runs.
x != 0 false. && SHORT-CIRCUITS — y/x never evaluated, no crash. else runs.

clozeSentence: Both conditions must hold; use the ___ operator
explanation: && requires both sides to be true.

clozeSentence: Logical AND uses the ___ operator
explanation: && chains two boolean tests.

clozeSentence: Two pass conditions joined by AND use the ___ operator
explanation: && gives logical AND.

spec: Write an if header that runs when x > 5 AND x < 20 (just the if line)
explanation: && joins two comparisons. Both must hold.

spec: Write an if-block that prints "in range" when x > 5 && x < 20. Three lines.
explanation: Range check using &&.

spec: Write a complete program: declare int score = 80, int attendance = 90, then print "pass" if score >= 50 && attendance >= 80 else "fail"
explanation: Two-condition pass/fail using &&.

prompt: Write an if-block that prints "in range" when x is greater than 5 AND less than 20

Write an if-block that prints "pass" when score >= 50 && attendance >= 80

prompt: By the same pattern, write an if that prints "pass" when score >= 50 AND attendance >= 80.

steps.annotation: || true if EITHER side is true. -5 < 0 true -> overall true
|| accepts EITHER day. day == 6 true -> overall true

teachMe: -5 < 0 true. || short-circuits true and right side may not run. Body runs.
200 < 0 false but 200 > 100 true. || true. Body runs.
Both sides false: 50 not < 0, 50 not > 100. || false. else runs.
day == 6 false, day == 7 true. || true. weekend.
day == 6 false, day == 7 false. || false. weekday.
a == 0 true. || short-circuits true. Body runs.

clozeSentence: Either condition makes it true; use the ___ operator
explanation: || is logical OR. True if either side is true.

clozeSentence: Match either of two values with the ___ operator
explanation: || accepts either match.

clozeSentence: Either a or b is zero; the operator is ___
explanation: || triggers when either side is true.

spec: Write an if header that runs when x is out of bounds: x < 0 OR x > 100 (just the if line)
explanation: || logical OR. Either side triggers it.

spec: Write an if-block that prints "weekend" when day == 6 || day == 7. Three lines.
explanation: Match either of two day values.

prompt: By the same pattern, write an if that prints "got zero" when a == 0 OR b == 0.

steps.annotation: ! flips false to true. Condition is true

teachMe: found is false. !found is true. Body runs.
found is true. !found is false. else runs.
ok is true. !ok is false. else runs.
empty is false. !empty is true. Body runs.

clozeSentence: Negate a bool with the ___ operator
explanation: ! flips a bool. !false = true.

code: bool valid = ___(x < 0);
clozeSentence: Negate a comparison with the ___ operator
explanation: !(x < 0) is true when x is NOT less than 0.

clozeSentence: Test for the opposite of ok with the ___ operator
explanation: !ok is true when ok is false.

spec: Write an if header that runs when found is false (using the ! operator on found)
explanation: !found is true when found is false.

spec: Write a line that stores the negation of (x < 0) in a bool called valid
explanation: Negate the comparison; valid is true when x is NOT less than 0.
